title: Panels with ventilation elements
abstract: A plurality of ventilation elements, vents, slots, or gap elements or similar features that are applied to, or machined into, one or more sides or faces of exterior siding products. When the exterior siding product is installed, the ventilation elements, vents, slots, or gap elements or similar features provide a built-in air and water gap to facilitate and increase water drainage and air circulation behind the siding or panel, thereby minimizing water storage in the space behind the product and lowering the equilibrium moisture content of the siding itself. The ventilation features are incorporated into the corresponding product in-line or during the manufacturing process, and do not require any further work during assembly or construction on-site (e.g., furring strips or other similar drainage mechanisms are not needed).
claims: 1. A panel product, comprising: a wood-based panel comprising a first face, a second face, a top edge, a bottom edge, a right edge, and a left edge; a first plurality of first raised ventilation elements with a longitudinal axis, said first raised ventilation elements projecting from the first face; a second plurality of second raised ventilation elements with a longitudinal axis, said second raised ventilation elements projecting from the first face; wherein the first plurality of first raised ventilation elements and the second plurality of second raised ventilation elements overlap at least in part, but the first raised ventilation elements and the second raised ventilation elements do not contact each other; and wherein the first raised ventilation features are oriented at an angle to the second raised ventilation features so that the longitudinal axis of the first raised ventilation elements is not parallel to the longitudinal axis of the second raised ventilation features.
